name: grill-me
description: Relentless one-question-at-a-time Socratic interview that stress-tests a plan, design, or decision BEFORE any code is written. Maps the decision tree, resolves the highest-impact unknowns first with a recommended answer on every question, explores the codebase instead of asking when answers are discoverable, surfaces dependencies between decisions, pushes back on risky or contradictory choices, tracks resolved-vs-open branches, and ends in a structured shared understanding. Planning-only. Use when someone says "grill me", or wants to poke holes in / pressure-test / red-team / pre-mortem a plan before building.
